issues.description:
# /security-scan

## Name
security-scan

## Purpose
Security pre-merge scan вЂ” checks the diff for secrets, injection vulnerabilities, auth gaps, and data-access controls before code lands in production.

## Trigger
Agent task prompt starts with `/security-scan` (e.g. `/security-scan Scan PR #55 before merge`)

## Required Input
- The PR URL, branch name, or diff to scan
- The tech stack and framework (for targeted checks)

## Steps
1. **Secret detection** вЂ” scan the diff for:
   - Patterns: `password`, `api_key`, `secret`, `token`, `private_key`, `credentials` in variable names or string literals
   - File patterns: `.env`, `credentials.*`, `*.pem`, `*.key`
   - Base64 blobs that look like encoded secrets
   - Any hardcoded strings matching common API key formats (AWS, Stripe, etc.)
2. **Injection prevention**:
   - SQL injection: raw string interpolation into queries (no parameterized queries / ORM)
   - XSS: user-controlled data rendered via `innerHTML`, `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, or equivalent without sanitization
   - Command injection: user input passed to shell commands
3. **Auth and authorization**:
   - All new write routes (POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE) require authentication
   - All data-access queries scoped to the authenticated user (no `SELECT *` without user filter)
   - No privilege escalation: user cannot access another user's resources
4. **CSRF protection**: mutation routes use CSRF tokens or SameSite cookie policy.
5. **Input validation**: external input validated at system boundaries (API routes, webhooks, form handlers).
6. **RLS policies** (Supabase): every new user-scoped table has row-level-security enabled and policies defined; verify RLS is not bypassed with `service_role` key in client code.
7. **Dependency audit**: if new packages were added, flag any with known CVEs or unusual permissions.
8. Post a security gate comment: pass/fail per category with findings.

## Output
Security gate comment:
- Secrets: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- Injection: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- Auth / authz: PASS / FAIL (findings listed)
- CSRF: PASS / FAIL
- Input validation: PASS / FAIL
- RLS (if Supabase): PASS / FAIL / N/A
- Dependencies: PASS / FAIL / N/A
- **Gate: PASS / FAIL вЂ” merge recommended / blocked**

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Full diff reviewed (not just changed files at a glance)
- [ ] Secret patterns checked with regex, not just eyeballing
- [ ] Every new API route checked for auth middleware
- [ ] RLS checked for every new Supabase table
- [ ] Gate verdict stated explicitly (PASS = merge OK, FAIL = merge blocked)

## Failure Handling
- Diff not accessible: request the diff or branch name.
- Ambiguous finding (may or may not be a secret): flag as "manual review required" and do not block unless certain.
- Known false positive pattern: document why it was cleared.

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Add OWASP Top 10 checks incrementally. Retire specific checks if the CI pipeline adds automated equivalents (e.g. secret scanning via GitHub Advanced Security).
issues.title: security-scan

issues.description:
## Purpose
This issue was created to demonstrate the updated `user-stories-to-issues` scaffold (v2, Jun 2026) producing SEO checklist items automatically in the Acceptance Criteria section for a web app build task.

## Input
- User story: "As a visitor, I want to view a public product listing page so that I can browse available items before registering."
- Architecture: Next.js + Supabase, publicly accessible product catalog, authenticated checkout flow.
- Labels: `frontend`, `full-stack`

## Expected Output
A working product listing page at `/products` with server-side rendering, proper pagination, and search.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional
- [ ] `/products` page loads and renders all active product records from Supabase.
- [ ] Products are paginated (20 per page); pagination controls visible.
- [ ] Client-side search filters products by name in real time.
- [ ] Unauthenticated visitors can view the listing; add-to-cart requires auth (redirect to `/login`).
- [ ] Loading skeleton shown while data fetches; empty state shown when no products match.
- [ ] Error state shown (with retry) if the Supabase query fails.

### SEO (auto-injected вЂ” required for all web app issues)
- [ ] Semantic HTML: `<h1>`в†’`<h2>`в†’`<h3>` hierarchy correct; `<nav>`, `<main>`, `<article>`, `<section>`, `<footer>` used semantically.
- [ ] Meta title: unique per page, в‰¤60 characters, describes the page accurately.
- [ ] Meta description: unique per page, в‰¤160 characters, written for click-through.
- [ ] Open Graph tags: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url` present on every public-facing page.
- [ ] `robots.txt` at `/robots.txt`: allows crawlers on public routes; disallows `/dashboard`, `/admin`, and all auth-gated routes.
- [ ] `sitemap.xml` at `/sitemap.xml`: auto-generated, covers all public URLs, noted for Google Search Console submission.

## Dependencies
- Supabase `products` table and RLS policies must exist (see architecture issue).
- Auth redirect middleware must be in place.

## Risk
Low. Read-only data display; no write paths on the public listing page.

## Definition of Done
- `/products` renders in a browser with real data.
- All functional and SEO acceptance criteria checked off.
- QA has run `seo-audit-checklist` against the deployed preview URL and posted a PASS verdict.
- No secrets in the client bundle (grep verified).
issues.title: [TEST SCAFFOLD] Product Listing Page вЂ” demonstrates SEO AC auto-injection

issues.description:
# /qa-check

## Name
qa-check

## Purpose
QA checklist before merge вЂ” verifies that acceptance criteria, edge cases, UI states, validation, and security basics are all covered.

## Trigger
Agent task prompt starts with `/qa-check` (e.g. `/qa-check PR #42 вЂ” user auth flow`)

## Required Input
- The PR URL or diff to review
- The original acceptance criteria (from the issue or PR description)
- The feature under test (component, route, or flow)

## Steps
1. Read the issue acceptance criteria and PR diff together.
2. For each acceptance criterion, verify it is demonstrably met in the diff.
3. Check UI states:
   - Loading state present (spinner, skeleton, or placeholder)
   - Empty state present (helpful message, not blank)
   - Error state present (user-friendly message, no raw stack traces)
4. Check form validation:
   - Required fields validated client-side
   - Appropriate error messages shown inline
   - Server-side validation errors displayed to user
5. Check edge cases:
   - Empty inputs / null values handled
   - Long text does not break layout
   - Network failure / timeout handled gracefully
6. Check mobile responsiveness (375px viewport at minimum).
7. Verify no `console.error` or warning-level logs in the happy path.
8. Verify no hardcoded credentials, API keys, or tokens in the diff.
9. Post a QA pass/fail checklist as a comment on the issue.

## Output
QA checklist comment with pass/fail per criterion:
- Acceptance criteria: [AC1] pass | [AC2] fail вЂ” reason
- UI states: loading вњ“ | empty вњ“ | error вњ“
- Form validation: вњ“ / вњ—
- Edge cases: вњ“ / вњ—
- Mobile responsive: вњ“ / вњ—
- No console errors: вњ“ / вњ—
- No hardcoded secrets: вњ“ / вњ—
- **Overall: PASS / FAIL**

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Every acceptance criterion addressed (pass or documented failure)
- [ ] All three UI states verified
- [ ] Form validation verified
- [ ] At least 3 edge cases checked
- [ ] Mobile layout verified
- [ ] Secret scan done
- [ ] Overall verdict (PASS/FAIL) stated clearly

## Failure Handling
- No acceptance criteria found: flag as "AC missing вЂ” QA blocked" and request them before proceeding.
- Cannot run the code: perform static analysis of the diff and note "runtime verification required."

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Add platform-specific checks (e.g. Supabase RLS, Arabic RTL layout) as the workspace's tech stack stabilizes.
issues.title: qa-check

issues.description: # /seo-review ## Name seo-review ## Purpose SEO audit and improvement for web pages вЂ” meta tags, structured data, heading hierarchy, performance hints, and crawlability. ## Trigger Agent task prompt starts with `/seo-review` (e.g. `/seo-review Review this landing page`) ## Required Input - Target page(s) or route(s) - Existing page code (HTML / component) - Site URL (if available, for canonical/sitemap verification) ## Steps 1. Audit metadata: - `` tag: present, unique, 50-60 chars - `<meta name="description">`: present, 120-160 chars - Open Graph tags: `og:title`, `og:description`, `og:image`, `og:url` - Twitter Card tags: `twitter:card`, `twitter:title`, `twitter:description`, `twitter:image` 2. Audit content structure: - Single `<h1>` per page, clear heading hierarchy (H1 в†’ H2 в†’ H3) - Keyword presence in H1 and first paragraph - All `<img>` elements have descriptive `alt` text 3. Audit technical SEO: - Canonical URL tag present and correct - Robots meta tag not accidentally blocking indexing - Structured data (JSON-LD): check for applicable schema types (Article, Product, BreadcrumbList, etc.) - Page speed hints: large unoptimized images, render-blocking resources, missing `loading="lazy"` - Sitemap readiness: page listed or listable in sitemap 4. Fix or document each finding. 5. Produce an SEO score card (pass/fail per criterion) and list of recommended fixes. ## Output - SEO score card: criterion | status (pass/fail) | note - Recommended fixes (with code snippets where applicable) - Updated code if fixes were applied ## Quality Checklist - [ ] Title tag present, unique, within length - [ ] Meta description present, within length - [ ] OG / Twitter Card tags complete - [ ] Single H1 per page; heading hierarchy correct - [ ] All images have alt text - [ ] Canonical URL tag present - [ ] Structured data added where applicable - [ ] No accidental `noindex` directives - [ ] Score card posted as comment ## Failure Handling - No page code provided: ask for the target. - Dynamic meta tags (SPA): note that server-side rendering or SSG is required for full SEO and flag as architectural recommendation. ## Improvement Rule Review after 5 uses. Add Core Web Vitals checks if performance tooling becomes available in the workspace.
issues.title: seo-review

issues.description: # /accessibility ## Name accessibility ## Purpose Audit and fix accessibility issues to meet WCAG 2.1 AA standard. ## Trigger Agent task prompt starts with `/accessibility` (e.g. `/accessibility Audit the login form`) ## Required Input - Target component, page, or screen - Existing code ## Steps 1. Audit against WCAG 2.1 AA checklist: - Semantic HTML (correct heading hierarchy, landmark elements) - ARIA labels on interactive elements that lack visible text - Keyboard navigation: all interactive elements reachable via Tab; correct focus order - Focus management: focus trapped in modals; restored after modal close - Color contrast: 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text / UI components - Alt text: all `` elements have descriptive alt; decorative images use `alt=""` - Form labels: every input has an associated `
issues.title: accessibility

issues.description:
# /redesign

## Name
redesign

## Purpose
Redesign the UI with modern, clean aesthetics. Apply consistent spacing, typography hierarchy, accessible color contrast (WCAG AA), and responsive layout.

## Trigger
Agent task prompt starts with `/redesign` (e.g. `/redesign The settings page looks dated`)

## Required Input
- Target component, page, or screen to redesign
- Existing code or screenshots (if available)
- Brand color palette or design tokens (if defined in the project)

## Steps
1. Audit the current UI: identify visual inconsistencies, spacing issues, typography problems, contrast failures.
2. Apply modern design principles:
   - Consistent spacing scale (e.g. 4px / 8px grid)
   - Typography hierarchy (heading sizes, weight, line-height)
   - Accessible color contrast вЂ” minimum WCAG AA (4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text)
   - Responsive layout (mobile-first breakpoints)
3. Refactor component markup and styles to implement the redesign.
4. Verify: run contrast checks, test at 375px / 768px / 1280px viewports.
5. Write a change summary listing every visual decision made.

## Output
- Redesigned component/page code
- Change summary: what changed and why (spacing, colors, typography, layout)

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Color contrast meets WCAG AA at all breakpoints
- [ ] Consistent spacing scale applied throughout
- [ ] Typography hierarchy clear (H1 > H2 > body > caption)
- [ ] Responsive at 375px, 768px, 1280px
- [ ] No inline styles introduced (use design tokens / utility classes)
- [ ] Change summary posted as comment

## Failure Handling
- No existing code provided: ask for the target before proceeding.
- No brand tokens defined: use a neutral, accessible default palette and note it.

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Expand breakpoints or add dark-mode guidance if the workspace adopts a design system.
issues.title: redesign

issues.description:
# slash-skill-invoker

## Name
slash-skill-invoker

## Purpose
When an agent task prompt starts with `/skill-name`, resolve the named skill from the workspace Skill Library (project 08) and prepend its instructions to the agent's context before the first tool call.

## Trigger
Any agent task prompt that begins with `/skill-name` (e.g. `/accessibility Build a login form`, `/seo-review Review this landing page`)

## Required Input
- The raw task prompt starting with `/skill-name`
- Access to project 08 - Skill Library issue list

## Resolution Rules
- Match `/skill-name` to issue titles in project 08 - Skill Library (case-insensitive, partial match OK)
- If multiple matches, prefer exact match then first alphabetical
- If no match found: proceed normally and post "Skill `/name` not found вЂ” continuing without it."

## Conflict Rule
Skill instructions win on format/approach; task prompt wins on scope/subject.

## Steps
1. Parse the prompt: extract the `/skill-name` token (everything before the first space, or the full string if no space).
2. Fetch all issue titles from project 08 - Skill Library.
3. Resolve: case-insensitive match `/skill-name` (without the `/`) against issue titles.
   - Exact match wins; if no exact match, take the first alphabetical partial match.
   - If no match at all: skip injection and note it.
4. Retrieve the matched skill issue body (the SKILL.md content).
5. Inject: prepend to the agent context as:
   ```
   ## Applying Skill: /skill-name

   <skill-instructions>

   ---

   ## Task:
   <original-prompt-without-slash-prefix>
   ```
6. Continue execution with the injected context.

## Output
- Agent task executed with skill instructions prepended.
- If skill not found: a note posted вЂ” "Skill `/name` not found вЂ” continuing without it."

## Quality Checklist
- [ ] Skill name parsed correctly (handles `/name`, `/name-with-dashes`, `/name_with_underscores`)
- [ ] Resolution is case-insensitive
- [ ] Exact match preferred over partial match
- [ ] Not-found case handled gracefully (no halt, just a note)
- [ ] Injected context uses the canonical format above
- [ ] Original task scope/subject preserved from the prompt

## Failure Handling
- Skill library unreachable: proceed without injection, note it in run log.
- Multiple ambiguous matches: pick first alphabetical, note ambiguity.
- Malformed skill body: inject raw content as-is, proceed.

## Improvement Rule
Review after 5 uses. Refine resolution logic if false-positives or false-negatives occur. Retire if platform adds native slash-command support.
issues.title: slash-skill-invoker

issues.description:
## Incident

All 5 runtimes on the `ceo` machine (device daemon `019e3f5c-072f-7d86-b729-76da01f81f04`) have been offline since **2026-05-29T20:01:08Z** вЂ” approximately 55 hours as of this audit (2026-06-01T03:03 UTC).

**Affected runtimes:**
- Claude (ceo) вЂ” id `3a3d63ed-c616-47a9-a62e-ade63b656f0a`
- Codex (ceo) вЂ” id `a1e8349a-0c10-4420-837e-6d81409addb2`
- Opencode (ceo) вЂ” id `10a474f4-4123-4cff-8a54-fca7e74fd88b`
- Openclaw (ceo) вЂ” id `5c5ce7c0-e0a4-40a2-9344-eee54c433353`
- Hermes (ceo) вЂ” id `dd93d5a2-aa2e-4514-bc55-b856212cd5b7`

**Impact:** The `ceo` agent's preferred runtimes are unavailable. Any issue routed to the ceo agent will fail to execute until the device is back online or the ceo agent is assigned an alternate runtime (the NewTech-VPS runtimes are healthy).

## Expected Output
All ceo runtimes back online, or ceo agent migrated to NewTech-VPS runtime.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `multica runtime list` shows no ceo runtimes offline >5m
- ceo agent successfully picks up and routes a test issue

## Dependencies
- Workspace owner (admin@newtechkw.com) must check the ceo device

## Risk
CEO orchestrator cannot route new app requests or enforce quality gates while offline.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] ceo machine runtimes back online OR ceo agent reassigned to a running runtime
- [ ] Verified ceo can pick up an issue
- [ ] Root cause documented in comment

---
*Detected by Factory Health Audit DEV-175 at 2026-06-01 06:00 KWT*
issues.title: P1: ceo device runtimes offline 55h вЂ” CEO orchestrator unavailable

issues.description:
You are working on an existing project called:

NewTech Command OS

Important:
Do NOT rebuild from scratch.
Do NOT create a new prototype.
Do NOT replace the current app with a generic dashboard.
Continue from the current codebase and upgrade it into a production-ready operations platform.

Current project context:

- Project name: NewTech Command OS
- Business: NewTech Kuwait operations platform
- Domain: electronics retail, car accessories, smart home, installation, delivery, maintenance
- Current stack: React 19, Vite, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, Express
- Current persistence: file-based JSON database
- Current backend file: server.ts is too large and contains most API routes
- Current modules already exist:
   - Dashboard
   - Orders
   - Dispatch Board
   - Technicians
   - Drivers
   - CRM
   - Warehouse
   - Payments
   - WhatsApp
   - Reports
   - AI Command Center
   - Audit Logs
   - Settings
- Current API endpoints already exist for:
   - health
   - bootstrap
   - orders
   - technicians
   - drivers
   - inventory
   - crm tickets
   - settings
   - whatsapp send
   - payment verify
   - mobile jobs
   - Shopify webhook
   - MyFatoorah webhook
   - AI command

Current critical issues to fix:

1. No real database. Current database is database.json.
2. No real authentication or auth middleware.
3. No Zod validation.
4. No rate limiting.
5. server.ts is too large and must be modularized.
6. No pagination. /api/bootstrap returns all data.
7. No webhook retry queue.
8. No structured logging.
9. No unit tests or E2E tests.
10. Some invalid Tailwind classes exist.
11. Package name is still react-example.
12. Mock secrets exist in mock data and must be removed.
13. Phone normalization is inconsistent.
14. No individual mutation loading states.
15. No production deployment setup.

Your mission:
Upgrade the existing NewTech Command OS from a strong prototype into a production-ready system.

Business rules:

- Shopify remains the source of truth for products, variants, prices, discounts, and online checkout.
- NewTech Command OS is the operations brain above Shopify.
- MyFatoorah payments must never be marked paid from frontend only.
- Payment verification must happen server-side.
- WhatsApp and external API secrets must never be exposed in frontend.
- Every admin action must create audit log.
- Every integration action must create integration log.
- Every order status change must create timeline event.
- Every webhook must be idempotent.
- Kuwait phone numbers must normalize to +965XXXXXXXX.
- Arabic RTL must remain first-class.
- Do not remove existing useful features.

Target production stack for this existing project:

- React 19
- Vite
- TypeScript strict mode
- Tailwind CSS 4
- Express modular backend
- PostgreSQL
- Drizzle ORM
- Zod
- JWT auth
- bcrypt
- RBAC middleware
- Redis
- BullMQ
- Pino logger
- express-rate-limit
- Vitest
- Playwright
- Docker
- GitHub Actions

# ==================================================

PHASE 0 вЂ” CODEBASE REVIEW

First, inspect the current codebase.

Output:

1. Existing files summary
2. Existing modules summary
3. Current API route summary
4. Current data model summary
5. Critical risks
6. Safe migration strategy
7. Files you will change
8. What you will not touch yet

Rules:

- Do not start by deleting working features.
- Do not do a full rewrite.
- Refactor gradually.
- Keep the app running after every phase.

# ==================================================

PHASE 1 вЂ” CLEANUP AND SAFETY FIXES

Do these first:

1. Rename package from react-example to newtech-command-os.
2. Remove fake secrets from mockData.ts.
3. Move all secret-like values to .env.example only.
4. Fix invalid Tailwind classes:
   - bg-zinc-105
   - bg-red-105
   - text-zinc-650
   - text-zinc-850
   - text-zinc-805
5. Add centralized config loader.
6. Add centralized logger using Pino.
7. Add global API error handler.
8. Add React ErrorBoundary.
9. Add loading state and error state for every mutation.
10. Add consistent phone normalization utility:
   - normalizeKuwaitPhone(input) => +965XXXXXXXX
      - reject invalid Kuwait numbers
11. Add audit log helper.
12. Add integration log helper.

Acceptance:

- App still runs.
- No fake secrets remain in frontend/mock data.
- No invalid Tailwind classes remain.
- Errors are handled consistently.
- Logs are structured.

# ==================================================

PHASE 2 вЂ” MODULARIZE BACKEND

Refactor server.ts into modular backend.

Create structure:

server/
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ app.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ config/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ env.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ db/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ index.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ schema.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ migrate.ts
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ seed.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ middleware/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auth.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ rbac.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ errorHandler.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ rateLimit.ts
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ validate.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ utils/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ phone.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ audit.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ integrationLog.ts
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ idempotency.ts
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ logger.ts
в”њв”Ђв”Ђ modules/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ auth/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ orders/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ technicians/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ drivers/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ inventory/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ crm/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ payments/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ whatsapp/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ shopify/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ zoho/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ ai/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ mobile/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ reports/
в”‚   в”њв”Ђв”Ђ settings/
в”‚   в””в”Ђв”Ђ audit/
в””в”Ђв”Ђ jobs/
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ queues.ts
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ workers.ts
    в”њв”Ђв”Ђ webhookJobs.ts
    в””в”Ђв”Ђ whatsappJobs.ts

Rules:

- Keep existing API behavior working.
- Preserve endpoint URLs initially.
- Move one module at a time.
- Do not break frontend API client.
- Keep backward compatibility.

Acceptance:

- server.ts is reduced or replaced.
- Routes are separated by module.
- App still runs.
- All existing endpoints still respond.

# ==================================================

PHASE 3 вЂ” POSTGRESQL + DRIZZLE ORM

Replace file-based JSON persistence with PostgreSQL + Drizzle ORM.

Add packages:

- drizzle-orm
- postgres
- drizzle-kit

Create database schema for:

- users
- roles
- permissions
- user_roles
- customers
- addresses
- orders
- order_items
- order_services
- order_timeline
- technicians
- drivers
- inventory_items
- inventory_locations
- inventory_movements
- stock_reservations
- crm_tickets
- ticket_messages
- payments
- whatsapp_templates
- whatsapp_messages
- audit_logs
- integration_logs
- settings
- ai_commands
- mobile_sessions

Migration rules:

- Preserve current mock seed data.
- Create seed.ts to load current mock data into PostgreSQL.
- Keep JSON fallback only for development if DATABASE_URL is missing.
- Never use JSON database in production.

Environment variables:

- DATABASE_URL
- NODE_ENV

Acceptance:

- Data persists in PostgreSQL.
- Existing pages load from database-backed API.
- Seed command works.
- No production path depends on database.json.

# ==================================================

PHASE 4 вЂ” AUTHENTICATION + RBAC

Add real authentication.

User types:

- super_admin
- operations_manager
- customer_service
- warehouse_keeper
- technician
- driver
- accountant
- marketing

Implement:

1. Login endpoint
2. JWT access token
3. bcrypt password hashing
4. Auth middleware
5. RBAC middleware
6. Current user endpoint
7. Logout endpoint
8. Mobile login for technicians/drivers
9. Route protection by permission
10. Frontend login screen
11. Remove role switcher from production mode
12. Keep dev role switcher only when NODE_ENV=development

Permissions:

- view_dashboard
- view_orders
- create_orders
- update_orders
- assign_orders
- cancel_orders
- view_dispatch
- manage_dispatch
- view_customers
- manage_customers
- view_inventory
- manage_inventory
- view_payments
- verify_payments
- view_whatsapp
- send_whatsapp
- view_reports
- view_settings
- manage_settings
- view_audit_logs

Acceptance:

- Protected routes require login.
- API rejects unauthorized requests.
- UI hides forbidden modules.
- Audit logs include user ID.

# ==================================================

PHASE 5 вЂ” ZOD VALIDATION + API CONTRACT

Add Zod validation for all API inputs.

Create schemas for:

- createOrder
- updateOrder
- assignTechnician
- assignDriver
- createTicket
- addTicketMessage
- transferInventory
- sendWhatsApp
- verifyPayment
- updateSettings
- mobileJobUpdate
- webhook payload validation

Rules:

- Reject invalid input with clear error messages.
- Normalize phone before saving.
- Validate enum statuses.
- Validate payment amounts.
- Validate order status transitions.

Acceptance:

- All mutation endpoints use validation middleware.
- Invalid data cannot mutate DB.
- Frontend shows validation errors properly.

# ==================================================

PHASE 6 вЂ” ORDER ENGINE HARDENING

Improve order workflow.

Implement:

1. Order state transition service
2. Payment state transition service
3. Timeline event on every order update
4. Audit log on every admin action
5. Stock reservation check
6. Appointment conflict check
7. Technician skill check
8. Driver availability check
9. COD rules
10. Warranty creation flow
11. Support ticket creation from order

Rules:

- Cannot assign delivery unless order is ready_for_dispatch.
- Cannot assign technician if skill mismatch unless manager overrides.
- Cannot complete installation unless technician started work.
- Cannot mark MyFatoorah paid manually without special permission.
- Manual payment override must create audit log with reason.
- Every state change must be idempotent-safe.

Acceptance:

- Order actions are safe.
- Timeline is complete.
- Audit log is complete.
- Business rules are enforced server-side, not only frontend.

# ==================================================

PHASE 7 вЂ” PAGINATION + PERFORMANCE

Fix unbounded bootstrap.

Implement:

1. Paginated list endpoints:
   - GET /api/orders?page=&limit=&status=&q=
   - GET /api/customers?page=&limit=&q=
   - GET /api/crm/tickets?page=&limit=&status=
   - GET /api/whatsapp/messages?page=&limit=
   - GET /api/audit-logs?page=&limit=
   - GET /api/integration-logs?page=&limit=
2. Keep /api/bootstrap only for dashboard summary and small reference data.
3. Add database indexes:
   - orders.order_number
   - orders.customer_phone
   - orders.order_status
   - orders.payment_status
   - orders.created_at
   - crm_tickets.status
   - audit_logs.created_at
   - integration_logs.created_at
4. Add frontend pagination controls.
5. Add search debounce.
6. Memoize heavy derived lists.

Acceptance:

- Bootstrap no longer returns all historical data.
- Large order lists perform well.
- Frontend remains responsive.

# ==================================================

PHASE 8 вЂ” WEBHOOK RELIABILITY + QUEUES

Add Redis + BullMQ.

Queues:

- webhookQueue
- whatsappQueue
- integrationQueue
- aiQueue
- reportsQueue

Implement:

1. Webhook ingestion endpoints respond fast.
2. Store webhook event with idempotency key.
3. Process webhook in background.
4. Retry failed jobs.
5. Add dead-letter handling.
6. Add queue status page in Settings/Admin.
7. Add integration logs for success/failure.

Shopify:

- orders/create
- orders/paid
- orders/updated
- orders/cancelled
- refunds/create

MyFatoorah:

- payment status webhook
- verify payment job
- signature verification

WhatsApp:

- send template job
- receive status webhook
- retry failed messages

Acceptance:

- Webhooks are idempotent.
- Failed webhooks retry.
- Admin can see integration failures.
- No duplicate orders/payments from repeated webhooks.

# ==================================================

PHASE 9 вЂ” REAL INTEGRATION PLACEHOLDERS

Prepare production-ready integration services.

Shopify:

- test connection
- sync products
- sync orders
- webhook signature verification
- map Shopify financial_status to local paymentStatus
- map Shopify fulfillment_status to local orderStatus

MyFatoorah:

- create payment link
- verify payment
- webhook signature verification
- never trust frontend redirect only

WhatsApp:

- WATI provider
- WhatsApp Cloud API provider
- send template
- message status tracking
- failed retry

Zoho:

- sync customer to CRM
- create Books invoice
- record payment
- create Desk ticket
- sync Inventory item

Gemini:

- server-side AI command
- no GEMINI_API_KEY in frontend
- save AI responses
- require approval before AI actions

Acceptance:

- Services are server-side only.
- Secrets read from env only.
- Missing credentials show mock mode.
- Every action writes integration log.

# ==================================================

PHASE 10 вЂ” FRONTEND IMPROVEMENTS

Improve UI without changing product direction.

Add:

1. Mutation loading indicators.
2. Toast notifications.
3. Better error messages.
4. Empty states.
5. Confirmation modals.
6. Better filter bars.
7. Pagination.
8. Better RTL spacing.
9. Better mobile responsive layout.
10. ErrorBoundary per main page.
11. Safe optimistic updates only where appropriate.

Keep:

- Existing NewTech premium black/white/gold style.
- Existing Arabic RTL direction.
- Existing pages and workflows.

Acceptance:

- UI feels production-grade.
- Staff can understand what is happening.
- No silent failures.

# ==================================================

PHASE 11 вЂ” FILE UPLOADS

Add upload support for:

- Proof of delivery signature
- Proof of delivery photo
- Before installation photos
- After installation photos
- Customer attachments
- Ticket attachments

Use:

- S3-compatible storage if configured
- Local dev storage fallback only in development

Environment variables:

- S3_ENDPOINT
- S3_BUCKET
- S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID
- S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
- S3_PUBLIC_BASE_URL

Acceptance:

- Files are not stored as base64 in DB.
- Attachments are linked to orders/tickets.
- Upload permissions are checked.

# ==================================================

PHASE 12 вЂ” TESTING

Add tests.

Unit tests with Vitest:

- phone normalization
- order status transition
- payment status transition
- technician scoring
- driver scoring
- webhook idempotency
- Zod validation
- audit log helper

E2E tests with Playwright:

- login
- view orders
- create order
- assign technician
- assign driver
- verify payment mock
- send WhatsApp mock
- create ticket
- inventory transfer

Acceptance:

- npm test works.
- E2E smoke test works.
- Critical business rules covered.

# ==================================================

PHASE 13 вЂ” CI/CD + DEPLOYMENT

Prepare production deployment.

Add:

1. Dockerfile
2. docker-compose.yml for local dev:
   - app
   - postgres
   - redis
3. GitHub Actions:
   - install
   - typecheck
   - lint
   - test
   - build
4. .env.example complete
5. README updated
6. [SECURITY.md](http://SECURITY.md)
7. [DEPLOYMENT.md](http://DEPLOYMENT.md)
8. backup strategy docs

Deployment target:

- Coolify / VPS / Railway / Render

Acceptance:

- Clean build.
- Docker works.
- CI passes.
- No secrets committed.

# ==================================================

PHASE 14 вЂ” MOBILE OPERATIONS APP PREP

Prepare API and plan for mobile app.

Mobile roles:

- technician
- driver
- warehouse_keeper
- operations_manager

Required mobile APIs:

- POST /api/mobile/login
- GET /api/mobile/me
- GET /api/mobile/jobs
- PATCH /api/mobile/jobs/:id/status
- POST /api/mobile/jobs/:id/complete
- POST /api/mobile/jobs/:id/photos
- POST /api/mobile/cod/collect
- GET /api/mobile/notifications

Rules:

- Mobile app must not store external API secrets.
- Mobile calls backend only.
- Technician can only see own jobs.
- Driver can only see own deliveries.
- COD collection creates payment + audit log.

Acceptance:

- API ready for Expo/React Native or Android app.
- Permissions enforced.

# ==================================================

FINAL QA

After completing the phases, run full QA.

Check:

- TypeScript strict
- Build passes
- No frontend secrets
- Auth required
- RBAC works
- DB migrations work
- Seed works
- Order rules work
- Payment rules work
- Webhook idempotency works
- Audit logs work
- Integration logs work
- Pagination works
- UI Arabic RTL works
- Tests pass
- Docker works

Final output:

1. Summary of completed phases
2. Files created/changed
3. Database schema summary
4. API summary
5. New environment variables
6. Remaining risks
7. How to run locally
8. How to deploy
9. Next recommended development milestone

issues.description:
## Input
From VENDOR BRIEF: Bolt.new вЂ” benchmark sweep May 2026 ([DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85)), the model-selection-per-task pattern is marked **Adopt**:

> "expose model choice at issue-creation time; fast/cheap model for boilerplate, Opus Max-tier per issue type"

Validation signal: bolt.diy (19.4k stars, exceeds Bolt.new's 16k) вЂ” provider/model flexibility is the top community-requested feature. devosnt's squad model already has natural task boundaries; this adds the routing layer on top.

## Expected Output
1. Issue-creation CLI/workflow updated to accept a `model_tier` field (`standard` | `max`).
2. Agent assignment logic (or issue template guidance) routes by tier: boilerplate tasks (scaffolding, CRUD generation, style passes) в†’ Standard; complex reasoning tasks (architecture, security review, QA gate, multi-system integrations) в†’ Max (Opus Max-tier).
3. Sprint Commander skill updated to set `model_tier` when creating sub-issues.
4. Documentation (CLAUDE.md or agent operating manuals) updated to reflect the new field.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `model_tier` can be specified at issue creation time.
- [ ] Default tier rules documented per task type (boilerplate в†’ standard, complex в†’ max).
- [ ] Sprint Commander creates sub-issues with correct tier pre-set.
- [ ] At least one example sprint shows the split in action (one standard sub-issue, one max sub-issue).
- [ ] No regression in existing issue-creation flow.

## Dependencies
- [DEV-76](mention://issue/ee26fb58-8f56-41fc-9543-ca2f5c230f85) вЂ” source brief (must be in_review or done first).

## Risk
- If `multica issue create` does not support a `model_tier` metadata key today, the initial implementation is a convention (metadata key) rather than a hard-typed field вЂ” acceptable as v1 until platform support lands.
- Over-classifying tasks as Max increases cost without quality benefit; default rules must be conservative.

## Definition of Done
- Acceptance criteria above all checked.
- Sprint Commander operating manual updated.
- Security review not required (no user-facing surface, no auth changes).
issues.title: Implement model-selection-per-task routing (Standard vs Max tier)

issues.description:
Run the daily factory health audit. Cover: (1) runtime health (multica runtime list) вЂ” alert if any offline >5m; (2) WIP discipline вЂ” flag any agent with >3 in_progress or any squad with >6; (3) stale issues вЂ” list any in_progress issue >24h with no comments; (4) orphan/zero-skill check вЂ” every skill assigned to в‰Ґ1 agent; every agent has в‰Ґ1 skill; (5) metadata hygiene вЂ” sample 10 done issues and flag any with stale waiting_on / blocked_reason; (6) cost вЂ” pull the current month spend per project and compare to budget (cost-budget-tracker); (7) error budget вЂ” pull yesterday's Sentry error rate, latency p95, uptime % per shipped app and alert on SLO burn. Post one consolidated comment on the created issue with: GREEN/YELLOW/RED status, the top 5 issues found, and one-line remediation per issue. Status the issue 'done' if all green; 'in_review' if YELLOW; 'blocked' if RED. Do not silently ignore failures.

---
*Autopilot run triggered at 2026-05-27 03:00 UTC. After starting work, rename this issue to accurately reflect what you are doing.*
issues.title: Factory Health Audit вЂ” 2026-05-27

issues.description:
## Input
`dashboard-screen-planner` covers dashboards. There is no equivalent for general screens (auth, settings, onboarding, marketing, modal flows). Every UX-architect run rebuilds the structure by hand.

## Expected Output
Skill `ux-screen-generator`. Input: screen purpose (one sentence). Output: a structured spec:
- Primary CTA + secondary actions
- Data shown (fields + format)
- Permission gate (which role(s) can reach this)
- Empty / loading / error state variants (mandatory)
- Mobile breakpoint (в‰¤ 375px) layout note
- RTL variant note (when target locale includes Arabic)
- Accessibility minimums (focus order, ARIA labels, contrast)
- Recommended components from the workspace design tokens

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Output is implementation-ready (a frontend-engineer can build directly from it)
- [ ] Reused by `prd-to-user-stories` + `dashboard-screen-planner`
- [ ] Includes the 3 mandatory variants every time

## DoD
Skill published; product-manager + ux-architect agents have it in their skill list.
issues.title: SKILL: ux-screen-generator вЂ” general screen spec beyond dashboards (auth/settings/onboarding)
